cards.title: Your AI Co-Pilot is Going Rogue: Time for an AI Watchdog
cards.thesis: Builders are discovering that their AI coding assistants, like Claude Code and GitHub Copilot, aren't always reliable or transparent. From unexpected charges to taking credit for your work without consent, these tools are eroding trust, creating a clear need for a layer that gives developers control and visibility into their AI interactions.

evidence.text: Users report that Claude Code refuses requests or charges extra if your commits mention 'OpenClaw', causing significant frustration.
VS Code v1.117.0 automatically adds GitHub Copilot as a co-author to commit messages, even for users who don't use Copilot, leading to anger over unrequested attribution.

Users are frequently experiencing Claude.ai downtime and API errors, receiving messages like 'Account is no longer a member of the organization associated with this token,' indicating reliability and access issues.
When a Claude Pro subscription expired, $40 in prepaid 'Extra Usage' credits vanished without warning or refund options, highlighting a lack of billing transparency.

cards.opportunity: Everyone's getting burned by AI coding assistants acting shady – Claude refusing to work or costing extra, Copilot secretly taking co-author credit. You could build a simple plug-in for VS Code or Replit that acts like an 'AI watchdog' (a tool that monitors and controls AI behavior). It logs every interaction, flags unexpected charges or auto-attributions, and gives builders transparent control over their AI, right when trust in these tools is at an all-time low.

cards.title: Beyond the MVP: Why YC Startups Are Hiring Growth & CS First
cards.thesis: Early-stage YC-backed companies like Gooseworks and Stardex are immediately hiring for 'founding' roles in growth engineering and customer success. This signals that user acquisition and retention aren't just scaling problems, but critical, day-one priorities for new products, even before they've gained significant public traction.

evidence.text: Gooseworks (YC W23) is hiring a Founding Growth Engineer, indicating an early focus on how to acquire and expand their user base.
Stardex is seeking a Founding Customer Success Lead, showing a foundational commitment to user satisfaction and retention from the very beginning.

cards.opportunity: Many early-stage founders jump straight into building, but YC companies are showing they prioritize setting up growth and customer success *from day one*. There's an opening for a hyper-specific, AI-powered 'Founding Growth/CS Assistant' that acts like a fractional expert. It could analyze a new app's feature set and target audience, then spit out a tailored 30-60-90 day plan for initial user acquisition experiments and customer feedback loops, integrating with basic tools like Stripe or Intercom to automate the data collection. This helps solo founders act like they've already hired their first growth and CS leads, without the headcount.
